data.title: HeartCore Regains Nasdaq Compliance, Avoids Delisting Risk
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

HeartCore Enterprises, Inc. ( (HTCR) ) has provided an update.

HeartCore Enterprises, Inc. reported that on April 20, 2026, it received written notice from Nasdaq’s Listing Qualifications Department confirming the company had regained compliance with the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market. The company had first been notified of noncompliance on May 6, 2025, and was granted two 180-day grace periods, and with this compliance now restored, its common stock will continue to trade on Nasdaq without interruption, removing a key overhang around potential delisting risk for investors.

The resolution of this bid-price deficiency, formally announced on April 22, 2026, closes a nearly year-long process in which HeartCore worked to meet Nasdaq’s standards and maintain access to U.S. public markets. Continued Nasdaq listing supports the company’s credibility as a U.S. listing advisor to Japanese clients and helps preserve liquidity and visibility for shareholders, reinforcing its positioning in the cross-border IPO consulting niche.

Spark’s Take on HTCR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HTCR is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance, especially negative operating/free cash flow and a sharp 2025 revenue decline, alongside a bearish price trend. Offsetting these risks are an extremely low P/E with a solid dividend yield and positive corporate actions (buyback authorization and investor-protection bylaw changes).

To see Spark’s full report on HTCR stock, click here.

More about HeartCore Enterprises, Inc.

HeartCore Enterprises, Inc., headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, is a consulting services firm focused on providing U.S. market listing support and related advisory services, primarily to Japanese corporate clients seeking to access U.S. capital markets. The company trades on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the ticker HTCR and positions itself as a specialist in IPO and listing consulting.

Average Trading Volume: 42,318

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $4.25M

data.title: BRP Sets Fully Virtual 2026 Shareholders’ Meeting for May 28 and Adopts Notice-and-Access Delivery
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from BRP ( (TSE:DOO) ).

On April 22, 2026, BRP Inc. announced details of its 2026 annual meeting of shareholders, which will be held virtually via live webcast on May 28, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. Eastern time. Holders of subordinate and multiple voting shares as of the April 16, 2026 record date will be entitled to vote on the company’s audited financial statements for the year ended January 31, 2026, the election of 12 directors, appointment of the independent auditor, and an advisory say-on-pay resolution on executive compensation.

The company is using Canadian notice-and-access procedures for proxy materials, directing shareholders to digital channels for the management proxy circular, financial statements, and related disclosures while allowing paper copies on request, a move that lowers costs and supports environmental goals. BRP outlines detailed instructions for registered and non-registered shareholders to vote and, where desired, appoint and register proxyholders to participate online, emphasizing that proper registration and control numbers are required for voting at the virtual meeting, underscoring a continued shift toward fully digital governance processes.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:DOO) stock is a Buy with a C$107.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BRP stock, see the TSE:DOO Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DOO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DOO is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by mixed financials: strong operating/free cash flow and returning revenue growth are offset by materially weaker margins and a still-high leverage profile. Technical indicators add a modest drag due to negative MACD and below-neutral RSI, while valuation is neutral with a ~23x P/E and low dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on DOO stock, click here.

More about BRP

BRP Inc., based in Valcourt, Quebec, is a recreational products manufacturer known for powersports vehicles and related products. The company targets a global consumer base of outdoor and motorsports enthusiasts, and its shares trade in both subordinate voting and multiple voting classes, reflecting a controlled shareholding structure.

data.title: Tenax Therapeutics Names New CFO to Support Growth
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tenax Therapeutics ( (TENX) ) has provided an update.

On April 22, 2026, Tenax Therapeutics appointed veteran healthcare finance executive Thomas R. Staab, II as chief financial officer, effective May 11, 2026, succeeding interim CFO Thomas A. McGauley, who will remain in a consulting role through the filing of the company’s March 31, 2026 quarterly report and assist with the transition into late May. The move brings a seasoned public-company dealmaker and biotech finance specialist onto the leadership team as Tenax scales its global registrational program for levosimendan and positions TNX-103 as a potential first-in-class therapy for PH-HFpEF, with Staab’s compensation package anchored by inducement equity awards that align his incentives with long-term shareholder value.

Under Staab’s employment agreement, Tenax granted him 10,000 restricted stock units and options to purchase 450,000 shares, with multi-stage vesting schedules tied to his continued service. The company also structured tiered severance protections, including enhanced benefits in the event of a corporate transaction, underscoring both the strategic importance of the CFO role at this stage of Tenax’s development and the potential for future corporate or strategic activity in a competitive cardiopulmonary drug development landscape.

The most recent analyst rating on (TENX) stock is a Buy with a $35.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tenax Therapeutics stock, see the TENX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TENX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TENX is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (pre-revenue, widening losses, and accelerating cash burn), which raises ongoing funding and dilution risk. Technicals are a meaningful positive with an established uptrend and positive momentum indicators, while valuation remains challenged due to negative earnings and no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on TENX stock, click here.

More about Tenax Therapeutics

Tenax Therapeutics, Inc. is a Phase 3, development-stage pharmaceutical company focused on novel cardiopulmonary therapies. The company holds global rights to develop and commercialize levosimendan, including its oral formulation TNX-103, which is being developed to treat PH-HFpEF, the most prevalent form of pulmonary hypertension worldwide and an indication with no approved therapies to date.

data.title: China Automotive Systems Posts Record 2025 Earnings and Sales on High-Tech Steering Growth
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

China Automotive Systems ( (CAAS) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 22, 2026, China Automotive Systems reported that 2025 results delivered record net sales of $765.7 million and diluted earnings per share of $1.42, driven by 17.6% revenue growth, a 33.2% jump in gross profit and stronger margins as EPS sales rose 25.5% and reached 41.5% of total revenue. Fourth‑quarter 2025 performance was particularly strong, with net sales up 21.4% year‑on‑year, net income to common shareholders more than doubling to $18.4 million and operating income rising 108%, supported by higher demand in China, expanding export markets and an improved product mix.

Management highlighted that increased R&D spending and product innovation in technologies such as R‑EPS, L2+ electro‑hydraulic steering for heavy vehicles and active rear‑wheel steering are reinforcing the company’s positioning as a tier‑1 supplier for global OEMs. The company also completed a redomiciliation to the Cayman Islands in September 2025, a move executives say should enhance global flexibility, generate cost savings and support its shift to a six‑month reporting cycle, while robust cash generation lifted year‑end cash and investments to $256.7 million and net cash to about $169.7 million.

Spark’s Take on CAAS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CAAS is a Outperform.

The score is driven by solid underlying financial performance with stable margins and manageable leverage, partially offset by negative free cash flow. Technicals are supportive with price above key moving averages and positive MACD. Valuation is a key positive given the very low P/E.

To see Spark’s full report on CAAS stock, click here.

More about China Automotive Systems

China Automotive Systems, Inc. is a leading Chinese supplier of power steering components and systems, with a growing focus on higher‑technology electric power steering (EPS) products. The company serves both domestic and international automakers, including major OEMs in North America, Europe, Asia, South America and the ASEAN region, supplying steering solutions for passenger and commercial vehicles.

data.title: Axe Compute Secures Record $260 Million AI Infrastructure Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Axe Compute Inc ( (AGPU) ) has shared an update.

On April 22, 2026, Axe Compute Inc. announced it has signed a 36‑month enterprise infrastructure contract worth approximately $260 million with an unnamed customer, marking the largest enterprise engagement in its history. Under the agreement, Axe Compute will deploy a dedicated cluster of 2,304 NVIDIA B300 GPUs, AI‑focused high‑speed storage, and 4.8 megawatts of N+1 redundant power in a single U.S. Tier 3 data center, with deployment targeted to begin in the third quarter of 2026 and options to renew beyond the initial term.

The contract, structured with a deposit, prepayment, and take‑or‑pay monthly payments backed by enterprise‑grade service levels, provides Axe Compute with meaningful multi‑year revenue visibility and underscores its strategy of offering dedicated, customer‑specified AI infrastructure rather than shared cloud capacity. Management frames the deal as evidence of a structural shift in enterprise AI procurement away from hyperscaler constraints, potentially strengthening Axe Compute’s competitive position as enterprises seek predictable, scalable GPU resources for foundation model training, fine‑tuning, and high‑throughput inference at scale.

Spark’s Take on AGPU Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AGPU is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by severe financial deterioration (major 2025 revenue collapse, persistent large losses, and multi-year negative operating/free cash flow). Technicals provide some offset with improving medium-term momentum (price above key short/intermediate moving averages and positive MACD), while valuation offers limited support due to negative earnings and no indicated dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on AGPU stock, click here.

More about Axe Compute Inc

Axe Compute Inc., traded on Nasdaq under the ticker AGPU, operates as a “neocloud” AI infrastructure platform focused on delivering dedicated enterprise GPU compute capacity at global scale. The company provides enterprises and AI developers with configurable hardware, geographic flexibility, and rapid deployment, including a Strategic Compute Reserve that converts reserved GPU holdings into on-demand, deployable AI infrastructure capacity.

Positioning itself as an alternative to traditional hyperscale cloud providers, Axe Compute emphasizes dedicated clusters, predictable pricing, and customer-specified locations and service levels. Its offerings are geared toward large-scale AI model training, fine-tuning, and high-throughput inference workloads, targeting enterprises that require performance, data-control, and reliability guarantees for mission-critical AI applications.

Average Trading Volume: 1,922,867

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $25.59M

Spectral AI ( (MDAI) ) has provided an update.

On April 17, 2026, Spectral AI’s board approved an employment agreement formalizing Vincent S. Capone’s role as Chief Executive Officer, reflecting his start date of February 9, 2026 and earlier appointment announced in February. The package includes a $500,000 annual base salary, an annual performance-based bonus opportunity of up to 100% of salary with a $250,000 minimum, continued 401(k) contributions, standard employee benefits, and participation in a forthcoming executive exit bonus plan tied to a future sale of the company.

Capone was granted 200,000 restricted stock units under the company’s long‑term incentive plan, with 50% vesting on April 1, 2026 and the remainder on April 1, 2027, and all equity vesting upon a change of control. The agreement maintains his at‑will employment status but provides for 12 months of salary continuation, extended benefits, and accelerated vesting of equity if he is terminated without cause or resigns for good reason, underscoring Spectral AI’s effort to secure and align its top leadership during a period that could include strategic transactions.

data.title: T. Rowe Price Deepens Alternatives Push With OHA Alignment
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

T Rowe Price ( (TROW) ) has issued an update.

On April 22, 2026, T. Rowe Price Group and its alternatives affiliate Oak Hill Advisors updated their evergreen operating arrangement, reinforcing OHA’s role as a leading alternatives manager to institutional, insurance, wealth, and retirement clients. The move underscores T. Rowe Price’s commitment to expanding its alternatives franchise and aligning OHA’s long‑term growth with the broader group’s multi‑asset offering.

On April 21, 2026, the board’s independent directors approved an amended and restated employment agreement for OHA CEO Glenn R. August, detailing continued employment, a $350,000 base salary, performance‑linked bonus eligibility, and defined severance protections tied to non‑compete and non‑solicitation covenants. The compensation committee also adopted an OHA 2026 Partner Cash Compensation Pool Plan, a Supplemental Compensation Pool funded up to $20 million annually from 2027 to 2030, and a Value Creation Incentive Plan, tightly linking partner and executive pay, including equity deferrals, to OHA’s fee revenue growth and fee‑related earnings performance.

Under these plans, August receives a 16.333% allocation from the 2027 OHA Partner Cash Compensation Pool and an 8.166% share of the 2027 Value Creation Incentive Pool, integrating his rewards with both annual cash flows and longer‑term value creation at OHA. The revamped compensation architecture is designed to retain key talent, sharpen performance incentives, and better synchronize OHA leadership’s interests with those of T. Rowe Price shareholders and clients as the group deepens its push into alternative investments.

The most recent analyst rating on (TROW) stock is a Sell with a $87.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on T Rowe Price stock, see the TROW Stock Forecast page.

New Found Gold ( (TSE:NFG) ) has shared an announcement.

On April 22, 2026, New Found Gold reported final 2025 drill results from the Dropkick Zone at Queensway, showing strong high-grade intercepts both west and east of the Appleton Fault Zone. The company has expanded the Dropkick mineralized trend to over 1.4 kilometres along strike and to depths of about 300 metres, with the high-grade domain demonstrating continuity and remaining open in multiple directions.

Drilling at Dropkick in 2024 and 2025, totaling more than 16,000 metres, will feed into an updated Queensway mineral resource estimate and technical report planned for the second half of 2026. The findings reinforce Dropkick as a meaningful growth target within the broader Queensway development, influencing future exploration priorities, resource growth, and mine planning alongside the company’s ongoing 2026 drilling and grade-control programs at other zones on the property.

data.title: Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals to Showcase EP-104GI Data at Digestive Disease Week 2026
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals ( (TSE:EPRX) ) has provided an update.

Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals, a clinical-stage biotech specializing in locally delivered, extended-release drugs built on its Diffusphere technology platform, is advancing candidates such as EP-104GI for eosinophilic esophagitis and EP-104IAR for knee osteoarthritis pain, with broader ambitions in gastrointestinal, joint and oncology indications. The company, which previously reported that its Phase 2b SPRINGBOARD trial of EP-104IAR met its primary endpoint, continues to build a diversified pipeline of long-acting formulations aimed at improving safety, tolerability and durability for approved and novel therapies.

On April 22, 2026, Eupraxia announced it will present four abstracts at the Digestive Disease Week annual meeting in Chicago from May 2–5, 2026, highlighting clinical data from its ongoing Phase 1b/2 RESOLVE trial of EP-104GI in eosinophilic esophagitis. The presentations will cover dose escalation, durability of histologic, endoscopic and dysphagia improvements, and optimization of dose and injection patterns, while a separate virtual investor event at DDW on May 4, 2026, will showcase key opinion leaders and emphasize EP-104GI’s potential to prevent esophageal strictures, underscoring the program’s strategic importance and visibility with both clinicians and investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:EPRX) stock is a Buy with a C$11.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals stock, see the TSE:EPRX Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Spire Divests Mississippi Gas Utility to Refocus Footprint
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Spire ( (SR) ) has shared an update.

On April 21, 2026, Spire’s subsidiary Spire EnergySouth agreed to sell all shares of Spire Mississippi Inc., its Mississippi natural gas utility serving about 18,000 customers via roughly 745 miles of pipelines, to Delta Mississippi Gas Company, an affiliate of Delta Utilities, for $75 million in cash. The deal, announced publicly on April 22, 2026, values the business at 1.4 times its 2025 rate base and is expected to close in the first quarter of Spire’s fiscal 2027, subject to approval by the Mississippi Public Service Commission and other customary conditions.

Spire plans to use the sale proceeds to fund infrastructure investments at its larger regulated gas utilities in Alabama, Missouri and Tennessee, refining its geographic footprint and sharpening its focus on bigger regulated markets. The agreement includes standard termination rights, a reverse termination fee of $7.5 million payable by the buyer under certain conditions, and the planned transfer of frontline employees to Delta to support continuity of service, signaling a strategic portfolio realignment for Spire and an expansion of Delta’s Mississippi presence.

The most recent analyst rating on (SR) stock is a Buy with a $102.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Spire stock, see the SR Stock Forecast page.

More about Spire

Spire Inc., listed on the NYSE as SR, is one of the largest publicly traded natural gas companies in the U.S., serving nearly 2 million homes and businesses. Its core operations include regulated gas utilities in Alabama, Mississippi, Missouri and Tennessee, alongside related businesses Spire Marketing and Spire Midstream focused on natural gas services and infrastructure.

Delta Utilities, backed by Bernhard Capital Partners, ranks among the nation’s top 40 natural gas providers with about 600,000 customers across Louisiana and Mississippi. Headquartered in New Orleans, it employs 875 people and emphasizes investment to deliver clean, safe and affordable natural gas with a strong focus on service reliability and safety.

data.title: TuHURA Biosciences Secures $50 Million Credit Facility
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

TuHURA Biosciences ( (HURA) ) has provided an update.

On April 21, 2026, TuHURA Biosciences, Inc. entered into a secured loan agreement with Parkview Holdings One LLC, an affiliate of its largest shareholder, establishing a revolving credit facility of up to $50 million maturing on April 21, 2031 at a 12% annual interest rate. The facility allows monthly draws tied to TuHURA’s expense budget and is expected to extend the company’s cash runway into early 2028 while funding ongoing clinical trials and development programs, including its Phase 3 IFx-2.0 program and TBS-2025.

As part of the transaction, TuHURA agreed to a $5 million commitment fee payable in stock subject to shareholder approval, an annual 1.5% facility fee, profit-based principal repayments once product sales generate consecutive quarterly net profits, and restrictive covenants on additional debt, investments, and certain corporate actions. The company also amended warrants held by the shareholder affiliate to extend their exercise period to 2031 and granted Parkview a low to mid-single digit royalty on future net sales of IFx-2.0–based products, reinforcing insider backing but adding leverage, asset liens, and royalty obligations that will shape the economics of any future commercialization.

The most recent analyst rating on (HURA) stock is a Buy with a $7.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on TuHURA Biosciences stock, see the HURA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HURA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HURA is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial fundamentals—no revenue, widening losses, and sharply worsening operating cash flow—implying continued reliance on financing. Technicals are a relative positive with price trending above major moving averages, but momentum is stretched (high RSI/Stoch). Corporate-event risk is elevated due to the Nasdaq minimum bid price notice, while valuation is difficult to support given negative earnings and no indicated dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on HURA stock, click here.

More about TuHURA Biosciences

TuHURA Biosciences, Inc. is a Phase 3 immuno-oncology company developing novel therapeutics to overcome primary and acquired resistance to cancer immunotherapy in patients with advanced cancers. Its lead innate immune agonist IFx-2.0 is in a randomized Phase 3 registration trial as an adjunct to Keytruda for first-line treatment of advanced or metastatic Merkel cell carcinoma.

The company’s pipeline also includes TBS-2025, a VISTA-inhibiting monoclonal antibody moving into Phase 1b/2 development in mutNPM1 relapsed/refractory AML, and Delta Opioid Receptor-based bi-specific ADCs targeting myeloid-derived suppressor cells. TuHURA is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker HURA and focuses on addressing resistance mechanisms that limit the durability and efficacy of checkpoint inhibitors and cellular therapies.

data.title: Rafael Holdings expands Alzheimer’s portfolio with MIT license
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rafael Holdings ( (RFL) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 22, 2026, Rafael Holdings said its subsidiary Cyclo Therapeutics signed an exclusive license with MIT for a U.S. patent covering the use of cyclodextrins in treating Alzheimer’s patients who carry the ApoE4 mutation. The deal expands protection around Trappsol® Cyclo™ in an ApoE4-positive population, where dysregulated cholesterol metabolism is thought to drive amyloid and tau pathology.

Rafael highlighted preclinical data indicating Trappsol® Cyclo™ can improve cholesterol transport between central nervous system cells, reduce tau pathology and enhance learning and memory in mice, potentially targeting underlying disease mechanisms. Executives framed the license as a transformative milestone that strengthens the company’s Alzheimer’s program while it awaits Phase 3 results in Q3 2026 from Transport NPC, the largest study conducted in Niemann-Pick Type C1, which could further shape Trappsol® Cyclo™’s role across multiple indications.

Spark’s Take on RFL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RFL is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by weak financial performance driven by large ongoing losses and cash burn, partially offset by low leverage. Technical indicators are mixed/neutral, and valuation is not supportive given negative earnings and no dividend data.

To see Spark’s full report on RFL stock, click here.

More about Rafael Holdings

Rafael Holdings, Inc. is a biotechnology company that develops pharmaceuticals and holds interests in clinical and early-stage companies focused on drugs and medical devices. Its lead candidate, Trappsol® Cyclo™, is being evaluated in a pivotal Phase 3 clinical trial for the treatment of Niemann-Pick Disease Type C1, a rare, fatal and progressive genetic disorder.

The company also maintains stakes in other clinical-stage and early-stage pharmaceutical developers as well as an orthopedic-focused medical device firm, reflecting a diversified portfolio in life sciences. Rafael’s strategy centers on advancing Trappsol® Cyclo™ while leveraging its broader holdings to participate in multiple therapeutic areas and technologies.
